CANDIDATE MODE (T14): You are ONE OF N PARALLEL CANDIDATE PLANNERS in a generate-N-then-judge round for plan-flow goal **G1** (project: fecalia, ledger root = /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia). You write NOTHING to any ledger and emit NO status token. Ground yourself READ-ONLY, then RETURN a candidate plan as a single fenced ```json block.

Grounding (read-only):
- fetch_item("goals", "G1") — the goal (wanbond: 2-WAN bonding tunnel, adaptive FEC, amneziawg-go + custom conn.Bind; implementation AND test harness).
- The authoritative project prompt: Read /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/fec-prompt.md.
- The answered clarifying questions Q1–Q8 on the questions ledger (fts_search ledger:questions milestone:M1) — ALL answered; their answers are binding constraints:
  - Q1: accept proposed thresholds (P1 failover recovery ≤3s; P2 bonded ≥85% of sum, 5G <1% of bytes while Starlink healthy; P3 at 5%/15% injected loss ≥95% lost data frames recovered w/o retransmit, FEC overhead ≤2× configured parity ratio; P4 adaptive total overhead ≤ fixed-FEC baseline for equal masking, residual loss ≤0.5% at steady 5% loss) — named constants in the harness.
  - Q2: netns+netem with root available locally (two namespaces + veth emulating Starlink-like 45ms jittery / 5G-like 64ms stable); real Starlink+5G + VPS exist for later manual production tests; include a short scripted manual checklist per phase.
  - Q3: GitHub Actions CI for lint+unit; privileged netns/TUN e2e tests LOCAL-ONLY behind a build tag.
  - Q4: module github.com/7mind/wanbond; latest stable Go; golangci-lint; Nix flake (dev shell with Go + iproute2/netem tools, package); git init in place.
  - Q5: single TOML config, explicit role=edge|concentrator field, inline secrets, enforced 0600.
  - Q6: linux/amd64 + linux/arm64 (CGO_ENABLED=0); systemd units + install doc at P1; no packaging beyond binary + nix.
  - Q7: structured logs from P0 + localhost Prometheus /metrics endpoint from P2; harness asserts P2–P4 acceptance against metrics.
  - Q8: FULL P0–P5 task DAG now, with an explicit P0-findings checkpoint task gating P1; P0 timeboxed ~2–3 days; later phases revisable via /cq:plan:follow-up.

Return EXACTLY this candidate-JSON shape (references by title/headline; each task's `milestone` names its work milestone):
```json
{
  "milestones": [{"title": "...", "dependsOn": ["<milestone title>"]}],
  "tasks": [{"headline": "...", "description": "...", "acceptance": "...", "suggestedModel": "sonnet|opus|haiku", "milestone": "<milestone title>", "dependsOn": ["<task headline>"], "ledgerRefs": ["goals:G1"]}],
  "rationale": "why this decomposition/sequencing"
}
```
Guidance: decompose along the P0–P5 phases (work milestones may map to phases or coherent slices); every task needs a testable `acceptance` (use the Q1 thresholds verbatim where applicable); make the test harness (netns/netem fixtures, e2e build-tagged suite, CI workflow) explicit first-class tasks; sequence dependencies honestly (scaffolding → P0 spike → checkpoint → P1 …); respect the prompt's NON-GOALS and the decided architecture (do NOT re-evaluate the base). Keep tasks at a grain one implement-worker can finish in one sitting; aim for the MINIMAL complete DAG, not padding.

Your final message: the fenced json block, nothing else of substance after it.

1	# wanbond — cq project prompt
2	
3	Paste into `/cq:plan`.
4	
5	---
6	
7	GOAL: Build `wanbond` — a single self-contained Go binary (runs on both edge and
8	concentrator) that bonds two unreliable, heterogeneous WANs (Starlink: low-latency,
9	jittery, intermittent obstruction loss; 4G/5G: metered, stable, variable latency)
10	into one resilient, DPI-resistant tunnel for GENERAL IP traffic, with adaptive FEC.
11	No FOSS tool combines transparent failover + aggregation + data-efficient adaptive
12	FEC; this fills that gap, tuned for the 2-link case.
13	
14	ARCHITECTURE (decided — do NOT re-evaluate the base choice):
15	- Embed amneziawg-go (github.com/amnezia-vpn/amneziawg-go, WireGuard fork with DPI
16	  obfuscation, same Noise crypto) as a LIBRARY: its device engine provides TUN I/O,
17	  Noise handshake, AEAD, rekeying, peer roaming, persistent keepalive — all UNMODIFIED.
18	- All bonding logic lives in a custom `conn.Bind` transport implementation UNDER the
19	  WG engine (precedent: Tailscale magicsock does transparent path migration this way).
20	  The Bind owns one UDP socket per path (bound to its source address) and operates on
21	  OPAQUE encrypted WG datagrams — it needs no crypto of its own for the data plane.
22	- Outer bonding frame wraps each WG datagram: (outer-seq, path-id, fec-group, flags).
23	  Additional outer frame types: RS parity, per-path probes, path control.
24	- Receive side: unwrap -> FEC recovery -> resequencing buffer -> deliver to the WG
25	  engine as if from a single socket. WG sees ONE stable virtual endpoint per peer;
26	  the Bind privately manages the real per-path endpoints and their roaming.
27	- Security model: inner WG layer is fail-closed (injection/decryption impossible
28	  without keys). Outer DATA headers are unauthenticated — forgery costs only bounded
29	  buffer/decode work (DoS-grade, acceptable). Outer CONTROL/PROBE frames are
30	  authenticated via a static pre-shared key from config (HMAC or AEAD — vetted lib,
31	  not hand-rolled). Outer wire format must carry no plaintext magic constants and
32	  look like high-entropy UDP (see requirement 6).
33	
34	CONTEXT / GIVEN:
35	- Edge = Linux box behind a router that ALREADY pins each path to a WAN (traffic from
36	  source IP A egresses Starlink, from source IP B egresses 5G). Path-to-WAN selection
37	  is SOLVED externally; the tunnel only binds one UDP socket per path source.
38	- Concentrator = small low-latency VPS, public IP, NATs tunnel traffic to the internet.
39	- Measured links: Starlink ~45ms (jittery), 5G ~64ms (stable). Start with 2 paths,
40	  design for N.
41	- Edge is sometimes used from hostile networks (hotels etc.) that block or throttle
42	  identified VPN protocols.
43	
44	PRIORITIZED REQUIREMENTS (in order — earlier ones must not regress for later ones):
45	1. TRANSPARENT FAILOVER: a TCP flow survives a WAN dying with NO connection reset.
46	2. DATA-THRIFT: never duplicate all traffic; keep metered 5G ~idle until needed.
47	3. BANDWIDTH AGGREGATION on demand (single flow can use both links).
48	4. FEC: Reed-Solomon parity that masks loss without full duplication.
49	5. ADAPTIVE FEC: redundancy scales with measured per-path loss (low when clean).
50	6. DPI RESISTANCE: on-wire traffic must not fingerprint as WireGuard. The custom
51	   outer framing already destroys the WG wire signature; keep it free of magic
52	   bytes / fixed offsets and expose amnezia junk/obfuscation params (Jc/Jmin/Jmax,
53	   S1/S2, H1-H4) as defense-in-depth. NOTE: amnezia 2.0 protocol MIMICRY
54	   (QUIC/DNS/SIP lookalike) is destroyed by our outer wrapping — treat it as out of
55	   scope; target is "unidentifiable high-entropy UDP", not protocol mimicry.
56	
57	FUNCTIONAL:
58	- TUN I/O, handshake, encryption, rekey: provided by the embedded amneziawg-go engine.
59	- Custom conn.Bind: per-path UDP socket bound to a source address; endpoint roaming
60	  per path; outer framing as above; MTU accounting for outer header + WG overhead
61	  (no fragmentation / ICMP black holes; MSS clamping guidance).
62	- Receive resequencing buffer (bounded window + timeout) for multipath reordering,
63	  applied BEFORE delivery to WG (its anti-replay window then never sees pathological
64	  reorder; verify window size gives margin anyway).
65	- Per-path quality measurement (RTT/loss/jitter) via PSK-authenticated probe frames
66	  + outer-seq gap accounting. WG keepalive is per-peer, NOT per-path — path liveness
67	  is entirely ours.
68	- Scheduler: active-backup (data-thrift) -> weighted aggregation under load, FEC-aware.
69	- RS FEC over outer frames: group + emit parity within a deadline; receiver recovers
70	  <=K losses/group. FEC operates on ciphertext — content-agnostic.
71	- Adaptive control loop: adjust FEC ratio + scheduler weights with hysteresis (no thrash).
72	- Path up/down + add/remove; survive edge public-IP change on any path (mobile).
73	
74	NON-FUNCTIONAL:
75	- Robust while mobile (path flaps, IP changes) — must never wedge; fail-closed security
76	  (carries 100% of traffic + holds keys); single static binary + systemd + simple config
77	  (WG keys/peers + amnezia params + path list + PSK); metrics/logging per path
78	  (loss/RTT/throughput/FEC-overhead).
79	- Keep the Bind implementation portable across amneziawg-go and upstream wireguard-go
80	  (API drift hedge: the fork lags upstream; swapping bases must stay cheap).
81	
82	PHASES (each independently shippable + verifiable):
83	- P0 (SPIKE, timeboxed) = embed amneziawg-go with a trivial single-socket pass-through
84	  Bind. Verify: tunnel passes traffic edge<->concentrator; measure baseline throughput;
85	  document conn.Bind contract pitfalls (batched send/recv semantics, GSO/GRO paths,
86	  Endpoint identity model, amnezia junk packets arriving at the Bind).
87	- P1 (MVP) = transparent failover (single active path + per-path roaming). Verify: kill
88	  the active WAN mid-SSH/download -> session survives, recovers within N seconds.
89	- P2 = aggregation + data-thrift policy. Verify: bonded throughput ~ sum; 5G bytes ~0 while
90	  Starlink healthy.
91	- P3 = fixed-ratio FEC. Verify: at injected X% loss, >=Y% recovered w/o retransmit; overhead <=Z%.
92	- P4 = adaptive FEC. Verify: overhead tracks loss; total data <= fixed-FEC baseline for same masking.
93	- P5 = DPI hardening pass. Verify: outer frames show no fixed bytes/offsets (capture +
94	  entropy check); amnezia params configurable end-to-end; nDPI/Suricata do not classify
95	  the flow as WireGuard.
96	
97	LIBRARIES (evaluate within the decided architecture):
98	- amneziawg-go (base, decided) | klauspost/reedsolomon (RS FEC, de-facto standard).
99	
100	KEY RISKS TO INVESTIGATE FIRST (P0 targets most of these):
101	- conn.Bind API impedance: batch-oriented ReceiveFunc/Send, GSO/GRO fast paths,
102	  Endpoint identity semantics when hiding N paths behind one virtual endpoint.
103	- amneziawg-go fork lag vs upstream wireguard-go (missing perf work / API drift).
104	- WG anti-replay window vs multipath reordering (expected fine at our pps + skew, but
105	  measure); rekey epochs — use OWN outer-seq, do not reuse the inner WG counter.
106	- Reorder-buffer tuning vs Starlink jitter; adaptive control-loop STABILITY (the crux);
107	  FEC grouping latency vs recovery; congestion/bufferbloat (need pacing?).
108	- Hostile networks that block UDP wholesale (hotels): no in-scope mitigation — document
109	  as a known limitation (TCP/TLS fallback transport is a non-goal for now).
110	
111	NON-GOALS: not a general SD-WAN product; no GUI; not >3 links initially; path pinning is
112	out of scope (done externally); no TCP/TLS fallback transport; no protocol mimicry
113	(amnezia 2.0 style); do not re-evaluate the base-library decision (kcp-go, quic-go,
114	plain wireguard-go were considered and rejected in favor of amneziawg-go + custom Bind).
115

# wanbond — cq project prompt

---

GOAL: Build `wanbond` — a single self-contained Go binary (runs on both edge and

jittery, intermittent obstruction loss; 4G/5G: metered, stable, variable latency)
into one resilient, DPI-resistant tunnel for GENERAL IP traffic, with adaptive FEC.
No FOSS tool combines transparent failover + aggregation + data-efficient adaptive
FEC; this fills that gap, tuned for the 2-link case.

- Embed amneziawg-go (github.com/amnezia-vpn/amneziawg-go, WireGuard fork with DPI
  obfuscation, same Noise crypto) as a LIBRARY: its device engine provides TUN I/O,
  Noise handshake, AEAD, rekeying, peer roaming, persistent keepalive — all UNMODIFIED.
- All bonding logic lives in a custom `conn.Bind` transport implementation UNDER the
  WG engine (precedent: Tailscale magicsock does transparent path migration this way).
  The Bind owns one UDP socket per path (bound to its source address) and operates on
  OPAQUE encrypted WG datagrams — it needs no crypto of its own for the data plane.
- Outer bonding frame wraps each WG datagram: (outer-seq, path-id, fec-group, flags).
  Additional outer frame types: RS parity, per-path probes, path control.
- Receive side: unwrap -> FEC recovery -> resequencing buffer -> deliver to the WG
  engine as if from a single socket. WG sees ONE stable virtual endpoint per peer;
  the Bind privately manages the real per-path endpoints and their roaming.
- Security model: inner WG layer is fail-closed (injection/decryption impossible
  without keys). Outer DATA headers are unauthenticated — forgery costs only bounded
  buffer/decode work (DoS-grade, acceptable). Outer CONTROL/PROBE frames are
  authenticated via a static pre-shared key from config (HMAC or AEAD — vetted lib,
  not hand-rolled). Outer wire format must carry no plaintext magic constants and
  look like high-entropy UDP (see requirement 6).

CONTEXT / GIVEN:
- Edge = Linux box behind a router that ALREADY pins each path to a WAN (traffic from
  source IP A egresses Starlink, from source IP B egresses 5G). Path-to-WAN selection
  is SOLVED externally; the tunnel only binds one UDP socket per path source.
- Concentrator = small low-latency VPS, public IP, NATs tunnel traffic to the internet.
- Measured links: Starlink ~45ms (jittery), 5G ~64ms (stable). Start with 2 paths,
  design for N.
- Edge is sometimes used from hostile networks (hotels etc.) that block or throttle
  identified VPN protocols.

PRIORITIZED REQUIREMENTS (in order — earlier ones must not regress for later ones):
1. TRANSPARENT FAILOVER: a TCP flow survives a WAN dying with NO connection reset.
2. DATA-THRIFT: never duplicate all traffic; keep metered 5G ~idle until needed.
3. BANDWIDTH AGGREGATION on demand (single flow can use both links).
4. FEC: Reed-Solomon parity that masks loss without full duplication.
5. ADAPTIVE FEC: redundancy scales with measured per-path loss (low when clean).
6. DPI RESISTANCE: on-wire traffic must not fingerprint as WireGuard. The custom
   outer framing already destroys the WG wire signature; keep it free of magic
   bytes / fixed offsets and expose amnezia junk/obfuscation params (Jc/Jmin/Jmax,
   S1/S2, H1-H4) as defense-in-depth. NOTE: amnezia 2.0 protocol MIMICRY
   (QUIC/DNS/SIP lookalike) is destroyed by our outer wrapping — treat it as out of
   scope; target is "unidentifiable high-entropy UDP", not protocol mimicry.

FUNCTIONAL:
- TUN I/O, handshake, encryption, rekey: provided by the embedded amneziawg-go engine.
- Custom conn.Bind: per-path UDP socket bound to a source address; endpoint roaming
  per path; outer framing as above; MTU accounting for outer header + WG overhead
  (no fragmentation / ICMP black holes; MSS clamping guidance).
- Receive resequencing buffer (bounded window + timeout) for multipath reordering,
  applied BEFORE delivery to WG (its anti-replay window then never sees pathological
  reorder; verify window size gives margin anyway).
- Per-path quality measurement (RTT/loss/jitter) via PSK-authenticated probe frames
  + outer-seq gap accounting. WG keepalive is per-peer, NOT per-path — path liveness
  is entirely ours.
- Scheduler: active-backup (data-thrift) -> weighted aggregation under load, FEC-aware.
- RS FEC over outer frames: group + emit parity within a deadline; receiver recovers
  <=K losses/group. FEC operates on ciphertext — content-agnostic.
- Adaptive control loop: adjust FEC ratio + scheduler weights with hysteresis (no thrash).
- Path up/down + add/remove; survive edge public-IP change on any path (mobile).

- Robust while mobile (path flaps, IP changes) — must never wedge; fail-closed security
  (carries 100% of traffic + holds keys); single static binary + systemd + simple config
  (WG keys/peers + amnezia params + path list + PSK); metrics/logging per path
  (loss/RTT/throughput/FEC-overhead).
- Keep the Bind implementation portable across amneziawg-go and upstream wireguard-go
  (API drift hedge: the fork lags upstream; swapping bases must stay cheap).

PHASES (each independently shippable + verifiable):
- P0 (SPIKE, timeboxed) = embed amneziawg-go with a trivial single-socket pass-through
  Bind. Verify: tunnel passes traffic edge<->concentrator; measure baseline throughput;
  document conn.Bind contract pitfalls (batched send/recv semantics, GSO/GRO paths,
  Endpoint identity model, amnezia junk packets arriving at the Bind).
- P1 (MVP) = transparent failover (single active path + per-path roaming). Verify: kill
  the active WAN mid-SSH/download -> session survives, recovers within N seconds.
- P2 = aggregation + data-thrift policy. Verify: bonded throughput ~ sum; 5G bytes ~0 while
  Starlink healthy.
- P3 = fixed-ratio FEC. Verify: at injected X% loss, >=Y% recovered w/o retransmit; overhead <=Z%.
- P4 = adaptive FEC. Verify: overhead tracks loss; total data <= fixed-FEC baseline for same masking.
- P5 = DPI hardening pass. Verify: outer frames show no fixed bytes/offsets (capture +
  entropy check); amnezia params configurable end-to-end; nDPI/Suricata do not classify
  the flow as WireGuard.

LIBRARIES (evaluate within the decided architecture):
- amneziawg-go (base, decided) | klauspost/reedsolomon (RS FEC, de-facto standard).

KEY RISKS TO INVESTIGATE FIRST (P0 targets most of these):
- conn.Bind API impedance: batch-oriented ReceiveFunc/Send, GSO/GRO fast paths,
  Endpoint identity semantics when hiding N paths behind one virtual endpoint.
- amneziawg-go fork lag vs upstream wireguard-go (missing perf work / API drift).
- WG anti-replay window vs multipath reordering (expected fine at our pps + skew, but
  measure); rekey epochs — use OWN outer-seq, do not reuse the inner WG counter.
- Reorder-buffer tuning vs Starlink jitter; adaptive control-loop STABILITY (the crux);
  FEC grouping latency vs recovery; congestion/bufferbloat (need pacing?).
- Hostile networks that block UDP wholesale (hotels): no in-scope mitigation — document
  as a known limitation (TCP/TLS fallback transport is a non-goal for now).

NON-GOALS: not a general SD-WAN product; no GUI; not >3 links initially; path pinning is
out of scope (done externally); no TCP/TLS fallback transport; no protocol mimicry
(amnezia 2.0 style); do not re-evaluate the base-library decision (kcp-go, quic-go,
plain wireguard-go were considered and rejected in favor of amneziawg-go + custom Bind).

{"item":{"id":"G1","milestoneId":"M1","status":"clarifying","fields":{"title":"wanbond: 2-WAN bonding tunnel with adaptive FEC on amneziawg-go + custom conn.Bind — implementation and test harness","description":"Plan the implementation and test harness for `wanbond`, per the full project prompt in `fec-prompt.md` (repo root — authoritative source; summarized here).

GOAL: Build `wanbond` — a single self-contained Go binary (runs on both edge and concentrator) that bonds two unreliable, heterogeneous WANs (Starlink: low-latency, jittery, intermittent obstruction loss; 4G/5G: metered, stable, variable latency) into one resilient, DPI-resistant tunnel for GENERAL IP traffic, with adaptive FEC.

ARCHITECTURE (decided — do NOT re-evaluate): embed amneziawg-go as a library (unmodified WG engine: TUN, Noise, AEAD, rekey, roaming, keepalive); ALL bonding logic (multipath scheduling, RS-FEC, resequencing, per-path telemetry) in a custom `conn.Bind` under the engine, operating on opaque encrypted WG datagrams. Outer bonding frame (outer-seq, path-id, fec-group, flags) + parity/probe/control frame types. Inner layer fail-closed; outer control/probe frames PSK-authenticated; outer data headers unauthenticated (DoS-grade risk accepted). Outer wire format = unidentifiable high-entropy UDP (no magic bytes).

PRIORITIZED REQUIREMENTS (earlier must not regress for later): 1. transparent failover (TCP flow survives WAN death, no reset); 2. data-thrift (metered 5G ~idle until needed); 3. bandwidth aggregation on demand; 4. RS-FEC masking loss without duplication; 5. adaptive FEC tracking measured per-path loss; 6. DPI resistance (no WG fingerprint; amnezia junk params as defense-in-depth; protocol mimicry out of scope).

PHASES: P0 spike (embed amneziawg-go + trivial pass-through Bind, document conn.Bind contract pitfalls, baseline throughput) → P1 transparent failover MVP → P2 aggregation + data-thrift → P3 fixed-ratio FEC → P4 adaptive FEC → P5 DPI hardening (capture + entropy check; nDPI/Suricata must not classify as WireGuard). Each phase independently shippable + verifiable.

TEST HARNESS is an explicit first-class deliverable: per-phase verification criteria are in the prompt (kill-active-WAN-mid-SSH survival; bonded throughput ≈ sum; ≥Y% recovery at X% injected loss with ≤Z% overhead; adaptive overhead ≤ fixed-FEC baseline; DPI classification checks). Expect network emulation (loss/jitter/reorder injection, e.g. netns + netem) to make these reproducible.

KEY RISKS (investigate first, P0 targets most): conn.Bind API impedance (batched send/recv, GSO/GRO, Endpoint identity when hiding N paths behind one virtual endpoint); amneziawg-go fork lag vs upstream (keep Bind portable to wireguard-go); WG anti-replay window vs multipath reorder (own outer-seq, never reuse inner counter); reorder-buffer tuning vs Starlink jitter; adaptive control-loop STABILITY (the crux); FEC grouping latency vs recovery; bufferbloat/pacing; hotels blocking UDP wholesale (documented limitation, no TCP fallback).

CONTEXT: edge = Linux box behind router that already pins source IPs to WANs (path selection external); concentrator = small public-IP VPS that NATs tunnel traffic; Starlink ~45ms jittery, 5G ~64ms stable; start with 2 paths, design for N.

NON-GOALS: not a general SD-WAN product; no GUI; not >3 links initially; path pinning external; no TCP/TLS fallback transport; no protocol mimicry; base-library decision closed (kcp-go, quic-go, plain wireguard-go rejected in favor of amneziawg-go + custom Bind).

LIBRARIES: amneziawg-go (base, decided); klauspost/reedsolomon (RS FEC).","sessionLogs":[".cq/logs/20260701-231505-aacec84bd6a7748f4.md"],"rawLogs":[".cq/logs/raw/20260701-231505-aacec84bd6a7748f4.jsonl"]},"createdAt":"2026-07-01T23:11:54.649Z","updatedAt":"2026-07-01T23:15:47.437Z","author":"fable-5","session":"0047802a-1b44-4fcc-8198-d12359610ad6"}}
